market_context: Worst semiconductor day in years: memory-crisis narrative (IDC forecasts a record -13% smartphone unit decline in 2026), AI-trade revaluation after reports Meta may sell surplus AI compute, and strong labor data cooling rate-cut hopes (Fed's Warsh reaffirming 2% target). Rotation is flowing into financials (all 32 banks passed the Fed stress test; JPM launched a $50B buyback + 10% dividend hike) and healthcare. Note: every current holding AND every pending order sits inside the correcting AI/semi complex — concentration is the #1 portfolio risk today.

position_actions.action: SELL 100% at market + CANCEL the buy-limit 2 @ $235
position_actions.reason: Three framework red flags stack up: outgoing CFO sold ~half of his equity (insider-selling rule: reduce at least 50%), extreme valuation premium (~66x forward earnings after +196% YTD), and a sell-the-news unwind following the June 22 S&P 500 inclusion. No earnings catalyst until late August, momentum broken (-24% from the June high, closed at its 20-day low, -11% today). It is the weakest holding — framework says cut the weakest first. The pending buy at $235 is averaging down into red flags, which the framework explicitly forbids.

position_actions.action: HOLD — keep your existing stop at $171
position_actions.reason: Your stop order at $171 (-1.9% from entry) is already placed — good discipline. Earnings Jul 29 with guidance already de-risked for memory headwinds ($9.2-10B revenue, automotive +50% YoY). QCOM is -28% from its June high, so the tight stop is appropriate; if it triggers on sector noise, reassess after the print instead of re-entering immediately. Targets if the bounce comes: sell 40% ~$190, rest ~$205.

position_actions.action: HOLD — set hard stop 12.90, first sell 16.30 (40%)
position_actions.reason: Best setup in the book: Q1 broadband revenue +43% YoY, backlog +87% to $582M, raised FY26 guidance, beat EPS by 70% last quarter, and earnings land Jul 27 — a textbook 3-4 week pre-earnings drift play. Today's -10% was pure sector contagion, and it still closed above its 50-day average (~13.66). Plan: stop 12.90 (below the 20-day low and the $13 shelf), sell 40% into strength near 16.30 (prior 20-day high) BEFORE the print, and size down to <=50% if still holding into earnings, per the binary-risk rule.

position_actions.action: HOLD — set hard stop 61.50; second tranche at $65 stays
position_actions.reason: The Meta surplus-compute report hit the whole neocloud group and raised real customer-concentration fear; the counterargument is CoreWeave's ~$100B backlog and European buildout. Your pre-planned second tranche at $65 sits right at the 52-week low ($63.80) — a planned add at major support is framework-legal. Combined hard stop 61.50 (a close below the 52-week low breaks the thesis). Recovery targets: sell 40% near $100 (the technical reclaim zone analysts flag), remainder near $118.

Catalyst-rich: 2026 revenue guide of >=$375M (~7x YoY), $150M+ of Q2 orders, the Mistral merger opening $1B+ of DoD IDIQ access, Cyberhawk acquisition closing in Q3, and the proposed $1.1B 'Drone Dominance' program lifting the whole complex (analyst targets raised to $18 and $25). But the stock closed at a 60-day low, -51% from its high — structure is broken, so risk must be defined: hard stop 6.85 (below the $7.00 shelf). First sell 40% at ~9.80 (50-day MA), second at ~12.00.

The memory-crisis headlines (IDC: record smartphone decline) are aimed at exactly this trade. MU is -23% from its high after a +260% YTD run, closed -5.4% today, and has no earnings until late September — no timer, no visible floor. Don't catch this knife at a round number. Re-entry idea is on the watchlist: $800-840 zone after stabilization (two green closes or a reclaim of the 20-day MA).

position_actions.action: CANCEL the buy-limit 30 @ EUR 5.80 (Defiance Memory ETF)
position_actions.reason: This ETF (DRAM.DE, EUR) made a fresh 52-week low today at 5.84 after -10% — your limit is about to fill on a falling knife. It also duplicates memory exposure you would already have via the MU watchlist and QCOM's handset business. Cutting redundant correlation is free risk reduction.

position_actions.action: CANCEL the buy-limit 10 @ $47 until researched (T212 lists it as CLA / Ouster)
position_actions.reason: Ouster fell -16% today to $49.94, so the fill is imminent — and there is no thesis on file. Framework rule: no entry without the 3-step research. Ask for a deep dive on OUST tomorrow if you want it; until then an unresearched 10k CZK fill is a coin flip.

position_actions.action: KEEP the buy-limit 2.05 @ $185, but know what it means (T212 lists it as YNDX / Nebius)
position_actions.reason: Nebius trades at $211 (-8% today), -30% off its high; $185 is a legitimate deep-pullback add level. But it is the same AI-infrastructure theme as CoreWeave — if both limits fill you are doubling one bet while the theme corrects. Acceptable if intentional; consider halving the order size otherwise.

new_plays.name: JPMorgan Chase
new_plays.catalyst: Q2 earnings + fresh $50B buyback (active since Jul 1) + rotation into financials after all 32 banks passed the Fed stress test
new_plays.risk_reward: risking ~4.1% to make ~3.6% (first sell) / ~6.9% (full exit) — ~1:1.7, below the usual 1:2 bar, so size modestly
new_plays.checklist_score: 4/6 (fails analyst-upside; R:R borderline — passes catalyst, valuation, sentiment, timing)
new_plays.thesis: The one trade moving WITH the current rotation instead of against it: banks just cleared the stress test with a record $708B loss scenario, JPM immediately launched a $50B buyback and raised the dividend 10%, sector trades under 20x earnings, and JPM showed clear relative strength today (flat while semis fell 3-11%). Q2 earnings Jul 14 (~$5.61 EPS expected) is the dated catalyst, with pre-earnings drift into it. Equally important: this is the first non-AI position in the book — it directly cuts portfolio concentration.
new_plays.risks: Net-interest-income guidance disappointment on Jul 14; a hot CPI print pushing yields in the wrong direction; low beta means modest upside — this is a discipline/diversification trade, not a moonshot.
